title: Signal Compass Tabs
prompt:
Create a production-ready Signal Compass Tabs pattern using TypeScript React, Tailwind CSS, and Framer Motion. This original navigation component has three tabs—Observe, Frame, Decide—arranged around a small cyan compass point. Selecting a tab moves the compass indicator and updates a concise panel with the current activity. Use a light Bento system: white card, #F4F5F6 support surface, #ECECEE border, 22px corners, a soft neutral shadow, #111111 type, and #4A4A4F secondary copy. Cyan is the only bright accent and the card must not use gradients.

Use tablist, tab, tabpanel, aria-selected, aria-controls, and a polite live message. Support ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Home, and End keyboard navigation in addition to click, Enter, and Space; every tab needs a 44px target and a visible 3px focus ring. Move the compass point with a spring at stiffness 350 and damping 27; fade panel text in 150ms ease-out. Keep local deterministic state only, without timers, random values, storage, remote assets, or network requests.

At 320px, retain three readable tabs in a compact row; at large sizes, allow a wider content panel without absolute page placement. Preserve logical focus flow, AA contrast, and words as well as the cyan point to communicate selection. With prefers-reduced-motion, update immediately with a short opacity fade. Return complete client code plus a runnable pure CSS approximation and suggested HTML.

code.css:
/* HTML:
*/ .signal-compass{display:grid;grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr);max-width:340px;border-bottom:4px solid #14b8c4}.signal-compass button{min-height:44px;border:0;border-radius:12px;background:#fff;font-weight:800}.signal-compass button[aria-selected=true]{background:#f4f5f6}.signal-compass button:focus-visible{outline:3px solid #111;outline-offset:3px}@media(prefers-reduced-motion:reduce){.signal-compass *{transition:none}}
